q: The five numbers 23, 8, 41, 15, and 6 form a data set. What is the range of this data set?
explain: The range is the largest value minus the smallest value: 41 - 6 = 35.

q: A list of 6 numbers has an average (arithmetic mean) of 22. What is the sum of all 6 numbers?
Consider the data set: 4, 7, 7, 7, 10, 13, 16. What is the mode?
explain: The mode is the value that appears most often. 7 appears three times, more than any other value, so the mode is 7.

q: What is the median of the data set: 11, 4, 19, 8, 16?
explain: Order the values: 4, 8, 11, 16, 19. With 5 (odd) values, the median is the middle one, the 3rd value: 11.

q: In a class, 18 students scored an average of 72 on a test and 12 students scored an average of 92. What is the average score for all 30 students?
A set of 8 numbers has an average (arithmetic mean) of 17. A ninth number is added to the set, and the new average of all 9 numbers is 19. What is the value of the number that was added?
explain: Original sum = 8 × 17 = 136. New sum = 9 × 19 = 171. The added number = 171 - 136 = 35.

q: The data set 5, 9, 9, 14, 16, 21, 21, 25 has 8 values. What is the median?
explain: The values are already in order. With 8 (even) values, the median is the average of the 4th and 5th values: (14 + 16) / 2 = 15.

q: A test has scores that are normally distributed with a mean of 500 and a standard deviation of 100.
info: In a normal distribution, about 68% of values fall within 1 standard deviation of the mean, about 95% within 2, and about 99.7% within 3.
qa: The percent of scores between 300 and 700
explain: 300 and 700 are exactly 2 standard deviations below and above the mean (500 - 200 and 500 + 200). By the 68-95-99.7 rule, about 95% of values fall within 2 standard deviations. Quantity A = 95% = Quantity B. The answer is equal.

qa: The standard deviation of Set X
qb: The standard deviation of Set Y
explain: Standard deviation measures spread about the mean. In Set X every value equals the mean, so there is no spread and its standard deviation is 0. Set Y has values spread out around its mean of 50, so its standard deviation is positive (greater than 0). Therefore Quantity B is greater. (No hand computation needed; this tests the concept of spread.)

q: A data set of 5 numbers has an average (arithmetic mean) of 40. If the number 60 is removed from the set, what is the average of the remaining 4 numbers?
explain: Original sum = 5 × 40 = 200. After removing 60, the new sum = 200 - 60 = 140 over 4 numbers, so the new average = 140 / 4 = 35.

q: A data set has a mean of 50 and a standard deviation of 8. Every value in the data set is then increased by 5, creating a new data set. Which of the following statements about the new data set must be true? Select all that apply.
choices: The new range is the same as the original range
explain: Adding the same constant to every value shifts the whole distribution up: the mean increases by 5 (50 + 5 = 55), so the first statement is true and the second is false. Spread is unchanged by a uniform shift, so the standard deviation stays 8 (third true, fourth false) and the range is unchanged (fifth true). Correct selections: the new mean is 55; the new standard deviation is 8; the new range is the same as the original range.

q: The 13 values below are listed in increasing order: 3, 7, 7, 10, 14, 16, 18, 19, 22, 22, 28, 30, 35.
info: The interquartile range (IQR) is the third quartile (Q3) minus the first quartile (Q1).
qa: The interquartile range (IQR) of the data set
explain: With 13 values, the median is the 7th value (18). The lower half is the 6 values below it: 3, 7, 7, 10, 14, 16; Q1 is the average of its middle two (3rd and 4th): (7 + 10)/2 = 8.5. The upper half is the 6 values above the median: 19, 22, 22, 28, 30, 35; Q3 is the average of its middle two (3rd and 4th): (22 + 28)/2 = 25. IQR = 25 - 8.5 = 16.5, which is greater than 15. Quantity A is greater.
